description: V2 length-matched conservation laws for 2D point-vortex system. Fixes 4 facts with ratio > 1.5.

facts.context: 2D point-vortex system, N vortices at positions (xi,yi) with circulations Gi, Kirchhoff equations. Which quantity is exactly conserved?
2D point-vortex system with circulations Gi at positions (xi,yi). Angular impulse (Kirchhoff 1876). Which is exactly conserved?
2D point-vortex system, Kirchhoff equations. Linear impulse x-component. Which is exactly conserved?
2D point-vortex system, Kirchhoff equations. Linear impulse y-component. Which is exactly conserved?
Two point vortices with equal circulations G1=G2. Kirchhoff equations. What is constant?
distractors: separation r12 increases monotonically during the co-rotation
angle theta12 = arctan((y1-y2)/(x1-x2)) stays constant always
separation r12 oscillates with period T = 2*pi*r12^2 / G total

facts.context: N point vortices with total circulation G_tot = Sum Gi not zero. Kirchhoff equations. What is conserved?
facts.truth: vorticity centroid Xcm = Sum Gi*xi / G_tot and Ycm = Sum Gi*yi / G_tot
distractors: geometric centroid Xcm = Sum xi / N and Ycm = Sum yi / N (unweighted)
linear impulse total Xcm = Sum Gi*xi and Ycm = Sum Gi*yi (not divided)
vorticity centroid Xcm drifts at velocity V = G_tot / (2*pi) steadily

facts.context: Two point vortices with opposite circulations G1=-G2=G, separation d. Kirchhoff equations. Translational velocity?
Two equal co-rotating point vortices, each with circulation G, separated by distance d. Angular velocity?
Point-vortex equations in Hamiltonian form. The canonical coordinates are (qi, pi) = ?
3-vortex point-vortex system. Is it integrable (solvable in closed form)?
facts.truth: Yes: integrable (Aref 1979), H + Lz + Px + Py reduce to quadratures
distractors: No: chaotic for generic initial conditions with three distinct vortices
Yes: only for equal circulations G1=G2=G3 in symmetric configuration
No: only the 2-vortex system is integrable; three-body chaos ensues

facts.context: Restricted 3-vortex: two strong G1=G2=1, one weak test G3=eps<<1. Approximately conserved quantity?
facts.truth: Q = r12 + eps*(r13 + r23) is approximately constant (weighted perimeter)
distractors: Q = r12 + r13 + r23 with equal weights is approximately conserved here
Q = r12 is exactly constant regardless of the weak test vortex value
Q = r12*r13*r23 product of all pairwise distances stays nearly constant

facts.context: N-vortex system with circulations Gi. For smooth f(r), which weighted sum is approximately conserved?
facts.truth: Q_f = Sum_i<j GiGj f(rij) is approx const
distractors: Q_f = Sum_i<j f(rij) unweighted approx const

facts.context: Vortex dipole (G1=-G2=G) plus test vortex G3 on dipole symmetry axis. Exactly conserved quantity?
facts.truth: Q = Sum_i<j GiGj*rij is exactly conserved (frac_var ~ 1e-15)
distractors: Q = Sum_i<j rij is exactly conserved unweighted on symmetry axis
Q = r12 dipole separation is exactly conserved as the only invariant
Q = G3*(r13 + r23) is the exactly conserved quantity on this axis
